Empowering Indonesia

Indosat is transforming and innovating to provide marvelous experiences and unlock infinite possibillities across the nation

#LebihBaikIndosat

#LebihBaikIndosat is born from our genuine care for what our customers need and feel in every moment of their digital lives. We continuously evolve through smarter and more relevant technology, more accessible services, and ways of working that are increasingly responsive to changing times.

Latest Update

Find update information from us

See More
Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison, Wadhwani Foundation, and Ministry of Manpower of the Republic of Indonesia Sign MoU to Develop 1 Million Indonesian Digital Talents through AI

Jakarta, May 19, 2026 – Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison (Indosat or IOH), the Ministry of Manpower of the Republic of Indonesia (Kemnaker RI), and Wadhwani Foundation signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) on Strengthening the National Employment Ecosystem on 5 May 2026 in Jakarta. This collaboration marks a strategic step to expand access to artificial intelligence (AI), digital skills, and entrepreneurship training for Indonesia’s workforce through the integration of the SIAPKerja ecosystem, Wadhwani Foundation’s learning platforms, and Indosat’s connectivity and technology ecosystem support. Through this collaboration, training will be expanded across a network of 24 Vocational Training Centers (Balai Latihan Kerja/BLK) under Kemnaker RI, achieved through the integration of Wadhwani Foundation’s AI platforms, JobReady and Genie AI, into Kemnaker RI’s SIAPKerja ecosystem. This integration will strengthen participants’ job readiness through more personalized career guidance, AI-driven adaptive learning, and industry-relevant skill modules designed to meet the latest workforce demands. In addition to supporting job seekers, this initiative also promotes digital entrepreneurship development as part of efforts to accelerate the commitment of Indosat and Wadhwani Foundation to empower 1 million digital talents and 100,000 Indonesian entrepreneurs through technology-based training.

Press Release
|
Tuesday, 19 May 2026
H3RO Esports 6.0 Tournament Series Concludes, Generating Thousands of Potential Indonesian Esports Talents

Jakarta, May 13th, 2026 - Over six years, Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison (Indosat or IOH) through its Tri brand has built H3RO Esports into one of Indonesia's leading gaming talent development platforms, engaging more than 42,000 participants nationwide. What began as a tournament has grown into a proven pipeline, producing players from rookie level all the way to the professional stage, competing both nationally and internationally. The latest edition of that journey, H3RO Esports 6.0: From Zero to H3RO – Rise of Rookies, has now concluded. H3RO Esports 6.0 drew strong participation, with 2,786 verified rookies registering from across Indonesia. The selection process was multi-tiered and developed in collaboration with pro teams. The H3RO Rookie Tournament opened proceedings, narrowing the pool to 210 top players, before cognitive and durability testing and in-game assessments by RRQ and HFX shortlisted 20 players. Those finalists then completed a Scrimmage Showcase, psychological evaluation, and IQ and EQ testing, with three top rookies from each pro team ultimately confirmed as ready to enter the professional pathway. Supporting the process was ChatGPT-based CoachGPT, which gave coaches an AI-powered reference point for evaluating player performance with greater objectivity.

Press Release
|
Thursday, 14 May 2026
Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison Accelerates AI at Scale, Delivers Strong Dividend Payout

Jakarta, May 5, 2026 – PT Indosat Tbk (“Indosat” or “IOH”; IDX: ISAT) announced the results of its Annual General Meeting of Shareholders (AGMS), marking a new phase of growth as the Company scales its AI North Star and delivers tangible value to shareholders. Amid a challenging macroeconomic environment in 2025, Indosat continued to demonstrate resilience through disciplined execution and a clear strategic direction, laying a strong foundation for long-term value creation. Vikram Sinha, President Director and Chief Executive Officer of Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison, said, “At Indosat, we believe every progress must lead to something greater. Our focus is clear: scaling our AI North Star to execute with discipline. This is how we transform performance into sustainable growth and consistently deliver tangible value to our shareholders, including through dividend distribution. This reflects our #LebihBaikIndosat spirit in empowering Indonesia.”

Press Release
|
Tuesday, 5 May 2026

Corporate Social Responsibility

Through its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) program, Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison is committed to empowering Indonesia in building a better future and maximizing the socio-economic value of our presence, especially in areas aligned with our core business in digital technology. In 2024, Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison disbursed a total of IDR 25.185 billion in CSR funds and reached more than 161,383 beneficiaries across Indonesia. This impact was delivered through our four CSR pillars—Digital Education, Community Empowerment, Environment, and Philanthropy—by leveraging our expertise and sustainable resources.

See More →
See More →
See More →
See More →

Digital Education - Since the beginning of Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison CSR program, one of our aims has always been to support digital literacy and education amongst Indonesians, including encouraging digital innovation.

See More →

Community Empowerment - Besides empowering customers through better products and services, many of Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison initiatives seek to empower women and support gender equality, particularly when it comes to digital literacy.

See More →

Environment - Throughout the development infrastructure, Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison continually strive to reduce carbon footprint. Our site designs are improved to be energy efficient and environmentally friendly.

See More →

Philantropy - Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison Philanthropy has distributed supports for communities in the form of free health services through Mobile Clinic, distributed natural disaster assistance and communication support through Disaster Emergency Response initiative..

See More →
